review_comment: Other models use two layers for the classification head. We sure we want only one?

Adding another layer for the classification head would make this head incompatible with `DialogRPT`, which is the reason this PR is done.

There is no other interesting pre-trained sequence classification GPT-2 model that I know of, but if we hear of one that uses a second layer later on, we can find a way to enable that.
This assumes padding is done on the right. Are we sure it's always going to be the case? Should it be in the model documentation?
Agree - I think we can add a couple of extra lines to make sure `sequence_lengths` is also correct when padding is done from the left.

This can be managed in the tokenizer, but there is currently nothing that can be seen from the model side. I think this should be added cleanly in a second PR, I can take care of it once this PR is merged as imo this should be added upstream directly in `configuration_utils.py`, and is out of scope for this PR. What do you think?
